en.key_1482019100:
Every digit in a number has a place value.
This means its position tells us how much it is worth.
en.key_1098797788: Digits to the left of the decimal point are whole numbers, and digits to the right are decimals.
en.key_1596733328:
Today we will focus on decimal parts.
They represent values smaller than one.
en.key_1030798127: Here we have 5 tenths of a whole.
en.key_1576573258: Next, 9 hundredths of a whole number.
en.key_2132556311: And here, 7 thousandths of a whole.
en.key_2022921664:
Use the place value names 
to aim for the right hole!
en.key_413638833: Start by reading as you normally would.
en.key_483924095: Next, when we reach the decimal point, we always say “and.”
en.key_2049422137: Then, read the decimal part like a whole number and add the place value of the last digit.
en.key_1985527660: Reading it all together, we get:
en.key_2109683873:
Remeber: 

The name at the end depends on the place value of the last digit in the decimal part.
en.key_1079416684: 2 hundreds is the same as multiplying 2 by 100.
en.key_1971493391: 8 tens is the same as multiplying 8 by 10
en.key_1503700232: And 5 ones is the same as multiplying 5 by 1
en.key_178080841: Next, the 6 in the tenths place means 6 divided by 10.
en.key_2080893306: The 7 in the hundredths place means 7 divided by 100.
en.key_640664925: And the 4 in the thousandths place represents 4 divided by 1000.
en.key_399447526: That’s our number in expanded form!
en.key_112144178: Remember, it can also be written like this:
en.key_1653570498: Nice work! Let’s use expanded form to hit the right holes!
en.key_1799175421:
Remember:

Compare numbers by using place value and breaking them into expanded form.
en.key_70856860:
Remember:

Compare numbers by using place value and simplifying the expanded form.
en.key_1054396009: Let’s find out using place value!
en.key_762668040:
10.52 is read as “ten and fifty-two hundredths”
 because the last digit is in the hundredths place.
en.key_808739170: But in “Ten and fifty-two tenths” the last digit is  in the tenths place.
en.key_1483830681: We can identify the number by using expanded form!
en.key_751088895: First, write it down using place value.
en.key_1077795868: So, "ten and fifty-two tenths" is different than 10.52!
en.key_1583347486:
